behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure can occur at the STT->summary handoff, but the behavior text is primarily about whether speaker labels are correctly produced/preserved from real speech. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP: this passes through a real speech-to-text model, and correctness depends on model output content (diarization labels), so stubbed transcripts can hide real failures. MULTI-STEP: not required to validate multiple model decisions in sequence if we assert at STT output and handoff payload. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow was considered but rejected because the behavior text scopes to preserving speaker labels, not validating a multi-agentic chain outcome.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real multi-speaker meeting recording and the real STT API; assert transcript segments include correct speaker labels and that those labels are preserved in the structured payload sent to summarization.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is parser logic on transcript text and output structure, not inherently a cross-module failure boundary. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP: no real model judgment required if transcript text is provided. REJECTION-GATE: Integration/Agentic were considered but rejected because behavior text says 'Transcript parsing extracts...' (local extraction logic), not live model quality or external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Test the parsing function with crafted transcript snippets containing explicit assignments; assert exact assignee/task extraction into the expected summary input schema.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is not only orchestration; it checks whether generated summary content covers each assignee. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real LLM generation quality and may vary run-to-run. A mocked model can always satisfy the condition and hide real omission failures. MULTI-STEP: can be validated with a fixed transcript and one real summarization step, so Workflow is not strictly required. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow was considered but rejected because behavior text targets one live summary output condition, not multiple agentic decisions chained with separate acceptance criteria.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a transcript fixture with known assignees and run the real summarization model; assert the output action-items list includes at least one item per assigned attendee.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is routing/mapping logic once decisions are already identified. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess dependency. LLM-DEP: no dependence on live model output quality if identified decisions are test inputs. REJECTION-GATE: higher levels rejected because the behavior text explicitly says 'routes identified decisions' (post-identification mapping), which is local deterministic logic.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the routing function with inputs containing identified decisions plus narrative text; assert decisions land in `decisions` and are not emitted in generic narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this checks semantic extraction/placement quality in live summary content, not just deterministic routing code. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on what the real model decides to elevate as decisions and where it places them; stub responses hide that failure. MULTI-STEP: can be tested with transcript input + one real summarization/evaluation pass, so Workflow is not mandatory. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because behavior text does not require validating a chain of multiple agentic stages, only final decision placement quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run real summarization on transcripts with known decision statements; assert those decisions are present in `decisions` entries and absent as only narrative mentions.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure can occur in handoff/order/state propagation across split->process->merge stages. REAL-INFRA: usually involves real file handling and external API calls. LLM-DEP: coherence of merged summary depends on model outputs per segment. MULTI-STEP: explicitly requires multiple agentic steps in sequence (per-segment processing plus merge coherence), which matches Workflow by definition. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) yes real infra likely, (b) yes model output quality matters, (c) yes multi-step chain; therefore Integration is too low.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute recording, run full segmented processing with real STT/LLM calls, assert sequential segment execution, no dropped segments, and merged summary coherence covering content from early/middle/late segments.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is explicitly end-to-end across multiple modules and stages. REAL-INFRA: requires real upload/network/API behavior. LLM-DEP: includes real transcription and summarization model outputs. MULTI-STEP: multiple agentic steps are sequenced across the full pipeline, so Workflow is the minimum boundary for real-failure detection. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered but rejected because behavior text includes both transcribe and summarize stages, making it more than a single-system boundary test.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the production-like pipeline on a real meeting recording from upload through structured delivery using real external APIs; assert automatic completion and valid structured output schema without operator intervention.
